channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Hudson River Lighthouse Preservation Efforts Underway
item.description: Federal engineers begin process to preserve historic Hudson-Athens Lighthouse, listed on National Trust's endangered list.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TikTok asks the Supreme Court for an emergency order to block a US ban unless it's sold
item.description: TikTok is asking the Supreme Court to step in on an emergency basis to block the federal law that would ban the popular platform in the United States unless its China-based parent company agrees to sell it.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to factory reset your Android
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how to securely back up and factory reset your Android to protect your privacy and data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What tech teams need to know about WebAssembly
item.description: WebAssembly, or Wasm, is an open standard that defines a portable binary-code format and corresponding text format for executable programs. Created and maintained by the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C), Wasm is designed to facilitate high-performance applications on web pages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Schumer calls for Mayorkas to ‘deploy special drone-detection tech’ in New York, New Jersey
item.description: “I’m pushing for answers amid these drone sightings,” Schumer posted on X. “I’m calling for @SecMayorkas to deploy special drone-detection tech across NY and NJ.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: How to delete multiple emails at once on Android
item.description: Cut through all the digital clutter and delete multiple emails from your Android simultaneously. Kurt the CyberGuy explains how it's done.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some software stocks may be even less desirable in a rebounding market
item.description: Some recent developments suggest the beleaguered software group of stocks may be coming back into favor, as I noted a couple weeks ago following positive responses to both Snowflake and Elastic.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: If Congress won’t regulate AI, Scott Wiener believes California must
item.description: Last summer, Scott Wiener found himself in an uncomfortable position for any California Democrat: openly quarreling with Nancy Pelosi and garnering support from Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Chinese phone hack victims haven't been notified: Report
item.description: AT&T and Verizon have reportedly not informed some hacking victims that they were specifically targeted.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: The top lawmakers on the House Select Committee on the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) told Apple and Google on Friday to prepare to remove TikTok from their app stores, as a potential ban looms next month. Committee Chair John Moolenaar (R-Mich.) and Ranking Member Raja Krishnamoorthi (D-Ill.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Microsoft .NET Community Toolkit backs partial properties
item.description: Microsoft has released .NET Community Toolkit 8.4, with support for partial properties in MVVM Toolkit source generators as well as new MVVM Toolkit diagnostic analyzers. The update was unveiled December 12. Source code for the .NET Community Toolkit can be found on GitHub.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk wants to turn SpaceX's Starbase site into a Texas city
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk 's company on Thursday sent a letter to local officials requesting an election to turn what it calls Starbase — the South Texas site where SpaceX builds and launches its massive Starship rockets — into an incorporated city.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Enterprise buyer’s guide: How to choose videoconferencing software
item.description: When most businesspeople think of videoconferencing software, the first thing that comes to mind is probably Microsoft Teams or Zoom, but there are many other choices, each with its own strengths.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google claims breakthrough with Willow quantum computing chip but no real-world use yet
item.description: Google’s announcement on Monday of its new quantum chip, called Willow, was full of eye-catching statements. For starters, Willow took less than a minute to perform a benchmark computation that would take one of today’s fastest supercomputers 10 septillion years to do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenSilver 3.1 brings XAML designer for VS Code
item.description: OpenSilver 3.1, the latest version of Userware’s open-source framework for cross-platform .NET development, has arrived with what the company calls the first-ever drag-and-drop XAML designer for Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code editor.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nokia expands data center SONiC support
item.description: Nokia is offering customers an alternative way to set up data center networks by expanding its data center fabric package to support the open-source Software for Open Networking in the Cloud (SONiC).
item.dc:creator: Network World
